taskDescriptions: Plan an independence-phase spec-hardening change (adversarial review) from docs alone
Plan the dual-control topology (Josh upstream lane + owner synced fork, both ship) from docs alone
Plan the fix(admin) dead sidebar Edit button + tree-read race hardening from docs alone
Plan adding a new data-wx-* binding kind end-to-end (builder+editor+validation+tests) from docs alone
Plan a new POST /api/admin/publish/schedule route spanning server+publish+admin-ui from docs alone
uniqueGaps: FIXED: glossary.md miscited Invariant 20 for 'never edit a slot' (and its Storage exception); correct number is Inv 19 (Inv 20 is the hand-synced-ports rule). Flagged by 4 of 5 agents; invariant numbers are cited as identifiers so load-bearing.

FIXED: CLAUDE.md dev-install said `pip install -e ".[dev]"` but its own mypy(wixy_server)/pytest(full)/`python -m wixy_server` lines need the `server` extra; changed to `.[server,dev]`. Flagged by 3 agents; README/testing/runbook already said [server,dev].
FIXED: CLAUDE.md E2E heading still said 'once milestone 7 lands' (stale; v1 complete). Removed.

FIXED: POST /api/admin/publish read as both synchronous-terminal ({version,sha}) and async-job (SSE stream, 409-running-job-id) with no reconciliation. Clarified in contracts.md and publish-pipeline.md: the POST blocks through run_publish and returns the terminal result; the SSE stream is a concurrent channel reading the single app-wide app.state.publish_job; a 2nd overlapping POST 409s.

FIXED: media.md wrote the staged filename as <hash8>-<slug>.<ext>, colliding with the reserved page-slug term (other docs use <name>); step 7 defines it as slugify(uploaded filename). Corrected to <name> with a note.
FIXED: glossary.md pointed to runbook.md for the projects/ca.json field shape, which runbook lacked. Added a Project registry field table to runbook.md.

FIXED: '7 load-bearing collections' (glossary) vs '8 collection item schemas' (architecture/builder) sat unreconciled in sibling docs. Added a reconciling clause (7 COLLECTION_RULES rows; footer-link is the 8th schema, applied by a validate special-case).
FIXED: README stated WIXY_PORT default 8000 and 9380 without a cross-note; added 'default 8000, prod 9380'.

FIXED (real gap about existing code): pages[].editable computation was undocumented. Documented in contracts.md + serving-and-overlay.md: editable = pages/<slug>.html exists on disk (a staged-unpublished page is false until publish, decisions/00024); the whole state read runs under tree_lock() so it never observes a template mid-replacement.

FIXED (pointer): the independence phase had only a thin architecture.md §7 note with no deliverable-to-spec-file mapping. Strengthened the pointer; docs/ai deliberately documents only the shipped engine and defers the unbuilt independence subsystems (fork-sync, standalone deploy, pluggable AI, backups, HTML guide) to spec/independence/ (its README/09-work-plan/KICKOFF are the entry points).

NOT A DOC DEFICIENCY (noted, not amended): the low-confidence probes surfaced details of features that do not exist yet — scheduled-publish internals (durable timer, rev-at-fire policy, envelope), a new binding-kind's display-vs-stored round-trip + format param + date determinism, and the full independence mechanisms. docs/ai documents shipped reality and correctly defers unbuilt work to spec/; documenting non-existent code would violate the no-placeholders and link-don't-duplicate rules.

taskDescriptions: Re-verify: plan the fix(admin) dead sidebar Edit button + tree-read race hardening (confirms the round-1 editable/tree_lock fix)
Engine probe: plan a read-only compare-two-published-versions admin view (route + reuse of restore/ledger diff machinery)
Engine probe: plan a media-pipeline change (upload rate-limiting + accept AVIF) exercising allowlist/filename/reference-scan/tests
uniqueGaps: FIXED: contracts.md anti-drift rule referenced a 'CLAUDE.md routes table' that does not exist (CLAUDE.md's documentation map points to contracts.md as the canonical table). Flagged by 2 of 3 agents. Reworded to name contracts.md as the canonical route table.

FIXED: the token <name> for a staged media file was overloaded — media.md/serving-and-overlay.md used <hash8>-<name>.<ext> for the disk basename (name = slugified upload filename) but /admin/draft-media/<name> for the preview URL (which serves the FULL basename). Verified via source (routes_admin_api.py:412 returns url=/admin/draft-media/<processed.filename>; the API 'name' field IS the full basename). Reworked both docs so <name> = the full basename <hash8>-<upload-slug>.<ext> everywhere.

FIXED: publish-pipeline.md over-claimed that get_publish_preview's diff 'matches restore's diff'. Source shows it is overlay-op-driven (iterates sorted(overlay.ops); kind from extract_bindings_map/_binding_kind_lookup) — a DIFFERENT mechanism from restore's two-tree structural _diff_content, though the op granularity coincides because the overlay already stores collections as whole arrays. Corrected the description.

FIXED (clarity): contracts.md cited decisions/00024 for the editable predicate while Inv 16/publish-pipeline/media cite decisions/00024 for media copy-before-validate. Verified against source: 00024 is the M9-slice-1 omnibus entry — decision 1 = media copy-before-validate, decision 4 = page duplicate/delete (why a staged-unpublished page has no template on disk => editable:false). Both citations are correct; made the contracts.md one precise (decisions/00024 §4).

CONFIRMED FIXED (round 1 carryover): the editable predicate (template-exists-on-disk, read under tree_lock) and the staged-media filename are now documented; the admin-race probe's confidence rose 7 to 8.

NOT DOC DEFICIENCIES (noted, not amended): gaps describing features that do not exist (upload rate-limiting incl. a 429 convention; Pillow AVIF decode support/version) or fine internal-reuse details below doc-tree granularity (restore _diff_content signature/content-source; the projects/ca.json media->MediaConfig field mapping; the client <input accept> allowlist; whether a numbered invariant should restate the editable predicate). docs/ai documents shipped reality and defers unbuilt work to spec/; documenting non-existent code would violate the no-placeholders rule.

taskDescriptions: Engine probe: plan an auth+deploy change (second CF Access identity + a prod startup assertion for unset AUD/team-domain)
Engine probe: plan adding a new theme token (--radius scale) end-to-end (builder theme.css + hand-ported TS + themeVars live preview + overlay + tests)
Engine probe: plan adding DELETE conversation to the AI chat (route + chats.json store + runtime map + SSE + admin-ui)
uniqueGaps: FIXED (rendering bug): runbook.md wrapped the env var `WIXY_CF_TEAM_DOMAIN` inside a backtick code-span across a line break, which markdown renders as `WIXY_CF_TEAM_ DOMAIN` (a space in the name). Rewrapped so the var stays intact.

FIXED (contradiction): glossary.md's theme.css :root example omitted `--shadow` (`{ --<color>; --font-<role> }`) while builder.md correctly includes it. Source confirms generate_theme_css emits --shadow. Added it to glossary.md.

FIXED (naming near-contradiction): `ChatStatus` denotes two different types — cmdchat.py:ChatStatus (the cmd-client live-status dataclass {activity,process_kind,handover_state} from get_status) vs chats.py:ChatStatus (the pending|ready|failed Literal). glossary.md conflated the Literal with the ChatRuntimeEntry runtime-map entry. Disambiguated: the runtime map holds ChatRuntimeEntry whose status is the chats.py:ChatStatus Literal, noted distinct from cmdchat.py:ChatStatus.

FIXED (precision): architecture.md §5's one-line settings.py summary listed '(port, CF Access, dev bypass)' but omitted WIXY_ENV, a real Settings field (fully listed in contracts.md §2 and load-bearing for the prod refuse-to-start logic). Added WIXY_ENV + AUD/team-domain.

FIXED (precision): testing.md attributed the Python-mirroring vitest test only to googleFonts.test.ts, but Inv 20 makes themeVars.ts<->generate_theme_css the governing pair for theme-CSS too. Noted both pairings (themeVars + googleFonts).

NOT DOC DEFICIENCIES (noted, not amended): gaps describing features that do not exist (chat/cmd-side deletion + per-conversation SSE cancellation on delete; the exact boot-refusal function name; the CF Access policy identity-shape; the hypothetical radius-scale's theme.json layout + validation vocabulary) or fine internal handlers below doc-tree granularity (the overlay handler that applies themeVars to :root; a chat_runtime remove helper; a chats.json concurrency guard beyond documented single-operator LWW). docs/ai documents shipped reality; cmd/CF-operational detail and unbuilt-feature design correctly stay in cmd's own docs / spec/.

taskDescriptions: Final-coverage engine probe: plan adding a new site page + regenerating the parity baseline (builder build/validate/CLI + parity harness)
Final-coverage engine probe: plan registering + bootstrapping a SECOND project alongside ca (registry/storage/install/bootstrap)
Final-coverage engine probe: plan gracefully surfacing a non-fast-forward upstream state (checkout/watcher/publish-preflight/state)
uniqueGaps: FIXED (scope clarity): the docs echoed 'per-slug state; nothing may assume one project' but never stated v1 SERVING is single-project BY CONSTRUCTION — app.py:create_app raises RuntimeError unless exactly one project is registered, and public routes/admin-UI/app.state.publish_job/tree_lock/watcher/WIXY_CF_ACCESS_AUD are all single-project. Refined Inv 1: the per-slug state/path layer is future-proofing; the serving layer assumes exactly one.

FIXED (reconciliation): a non-fast-forward upstream was described as both a 'hard CheckoutError' (glossary/Inv 8) and 'degrades gracefully' (watcher) with no reconciliation. Clarified in serving-and-overlay.md: it is hard at ensure_checkout, caught+softened by the watcher (records WatcherStatus.last_error, keeps last-good sha, never forces), but propagated hard as PublishError('pulling') during a publish.

FIXED (precision): contracts.md §3 'CheckoutError -> 503 (->502 inside publish)' implied a raw CheckoutError reaches the publish handler as 502; source shows ensure_checkout's CheckoutError is WRAPPED as PublishError('pulling'). Corrected the mapping wording.

FIXED (accuracy): testing.md listed tests/parity/test_parity.py under 'hermetic — against the mini-site fixture', but the parity baseline is captured from the REAL CA site build (builder/tests/parity/baseline/ holds the CA pages), not the mini-site. Fixed the section header and the parity row.

FIXED (clarity): builder page discovery = load_site_source globs pages/*.html (a template alone registers a page; a present-but-meta-less content file fails validate, Inv 5) was not stated. Added to builder.md.

FIXED (small): clarified bootstrap = version 0 (bootstrap.py:BOOTSTRAP_VERSION) vs 1-based real publishes (so it does not read as contradicting Inv 11's next=max+1), and restored media.md's staged path to the generic <slug> form (a round-2 edit had baked in 'ca').

NOT DOC DEFICIENCIES (noted, not amended): gaps about multi-project SERVING (a v1 non-goal — spec/00 'UI stays single-project'); per-page indexability (project-level by design); internal helper signatures/fields (WatcherStatus fields, CheckoutError classification, the upstream wire assembler, the parity --slugs list location); and remediation for a diverged checkout. Correctly absent (v1 non-goals) or below doc-tree granularity.
